growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: nielsensl.
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.lvlwhy: the source paper (often a review/modeling reuse) states no strain
growth_records.medium: Natural seawater (North Pacific Ocean surface field samples); no defined culture medium — picocyanobacterial growth/mortality rates are field/in situ, with culture-based reference values cited to literature
growth_records.cite: Carlson et al. Viruses affect picocyanobacterial abundance and biogeography in the North Pacific Ocean. Nature Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41564-022-01088-x. [PMC8975747]
growth_records.snippet: Given that Prochlorococcus is estimated to double every 2.8 ± 0.8 d at the low temperatures in this region (ref. 12), we estimate that 21-51% of the population was infected and killed in the interval before cell division
growth_records.notes: Literature-derived assumption cited from ref. 12 (not a direct measurement by the present field study) used to model virus-induced mortality; 'low temperatures in this region' refers to the cooler part of the North Pacific transition zone studied (exact °C not given). doubling_time_h = 2.8 d x 24 = 67.2 h; growth_rate_per_h = ln2/67.2 = 0.0103 h^-1. No defined culture medium (in situ marine population). [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature, medium]

growth_records.medium: modified AMP-A (autoclaved artificial seawater, N-replete), with 5 mM TAPs
growth_records.cite: Szul et al. Carbon Fate and Flux in Prochlorococcus under Nitrogen Limitation. mSystems (2019). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00254-18. [PMC6392094]
growth_records.snippet: 0.2 liters culture was transferred ... to four identical bottles containing 2.0 liters medium (2.2 liters final volume) and incubated for 4 days to reach log phase growth (mu ~ 0.44); the experiment then began on day 0 ...
growth_records.notes: Log-phase specific growth rate reported as mu ~ 0.44 (units day^-1, inferred from paper's consistent use of day^-1: chemostat 0.09 day^-1 and 'one cell doubling per day' = 0.69 day^-1). Converted to per-hour: mu = 0.44/24 = 0.01833 h^-1. Doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.44 = 1.575 day * 24 = 37.8 h (equivalently 0.6931/0.01833 = 37.8 h). Medium: AMP-A is autoclaved artificial seawater derived from AMP1 (ref 51) with (NH4)2SO4 400 uM replaced by NH4Cl 800 uM; experiment used modified AMP-A with 5 mM Tris-acetate-phosphates; inorganic carbon (bicarbonate) standard AMP starts at 0.6 mM. Full base-salt recipe not itemized in text (given by reference to AMP1/Turks Island Salt Mix), so composition list is partial. uptake_rates/secretion_rates left empty: total organic carbon (TOC) and particulate organic carbon (POC) 14C-photosynthesis (inorganic carbon fixation) rates and DOC/exudate release were measured but reported ONLY as bar graphs in Fig 1 relative comparisons (paper has 0 data tables); no absolute mmol/gDW/h or per-cell numeric rate is given in text, so no qS(HCO3-) or qP could be computed without inventing numbers. Qualitatively: N-replete noon carbon fixation was the higher (reference) condition, dropping >50% by 16:00.

growth_records.medium: modified N-limited AMP-A (artificial seawater, 5 mM TAPs, 16 uM NH4Cl); N-free basal = Turks Island Salt Mix
growth_records.cite: Szul et al. Carbon Fate and Flux in Prochlorococcus under Nitrogen Limitation. mSystems (2019). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00254-18. [PMC6392094]
growth_records.snippet: the dilution rate of the chemostat (which in steady state is the growth rate of the population) was well below the rate for one cell doubling per day (0.09 versus 0.69 day-1) ... Cultures ... destructively sampled 6 days after chemostat dil
growth_records.notes: Chemostat dilution rate D ~ 0.09 day^-1 explicitly stated (replacing ~9% culture volume daily; also Fig S3: growth balanced to ~0.09 day^-1). At steady state mu = D, so mu = 0.09 day^-1 = 0.09/24 = 0.00375 h^-1. Doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.09 = 7.70 day * 24 = 184.9 h (equivalently 0.6931/0.00375 = 184.8 h). Paper explicitly contrasts this with 'one cell doubling per day' = 0.69 day^-1 (ln2 per day), confirming day^-1 units. Limiting N: 16 uM NH4Cl (vs 800 uM in replete). uptake_rates/secretion_rates empty: N-limited total carbon fixation reported as <60% of replete at noon and total photosynthesis <half of replete, plus larger fraction of fixed carbon released as DOC/exudate - but all reported ONLY as relative Fig 1 bar graphs (0 data tables); no absolute mmol/gDW/h or per-cell numeric flux stated, so qS(HCO3-)/qP not computable without fabricating values.

growth_records.strain: Prochlorococcus sp. MIT9313; MIT9312 (axenic); MIT0604 (axenic); NATL2A (axenic); MED4 (axenic)

sec.units: pmol CH4 per 10^6 cells per h (combined day/night average ~9; overall range 0.8-110)
sec.method: MIMS continuous measurement; slope of CH4 profile normalized to cell counts

growth_records.cite: Bižić M. et al. Aquatic and terrestrial cyanobacteria produce methane. Science Advances (2020). DOI:10.1126/sciadv.aax5343. [PMC6962044]
growth_records.snippet: Prochlorococcus cultures produced CH4 at rates ranging from 0.8 to 110 pmol CH4 per 106 cells hour−1 ... the combined day and night average CH4 production rates of high and low light Prochlorococcus species (~9 pmol CH4 per 10−6 cells hour−
growth_records.notes: CH4 secretion rate range 0.8-110 pmol CH4 (10^6 cells)^-1 h^-1; combined day+night average ~9 pmol CH4 (10^6 cells)^-1 h^-1 (value used as representative rate). Aggregate across 5 marine Prochlorococcus strains; no per-strain value in text. Cell-count normalized due to small cell size. Media AMP1/Pro99/FSW cited by reference, composition not stated. Temperature 22°C (Table 1). No growth rate reported; CH4 requires light >20 µmol quanta m-2 s-1.
